evals.name: VHT SU with Duration Target
evals.description: Tests duration targeting with wlanAPEPLength, correct sample rate usage, and plotting
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11ac (VHT) single-user waveform at 80 MHz bandwidth, MCS 7, with 2 spatial streams and 2 transmit antennas. The packet should be exactly 1 ms long. Use random payload bits. Plot the time-domain waveform magnitude in dBm (assuming 50 ohm impedance) with a proper time axis in microseconds. Print the sample rate, APEP length, and PSDU length.

assertions.description: Uses wlanAPEPLength for duration targeting instead of manual calculation
Does not hardcode sample rate
Generated code runs without error
Uses wlanSampleRate(cfg) for sample rate
Duration argument is integer microseconds (1000), not seconds
Single-user payload is a plain vector, not wrapped in a cell array

evals.name: HE-MU OFDMA 80 MHz
evals.description: Tests HE-MU allocation index knowledge, per-user config, getPSDULength, and showAllocation
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11ax HE-MU OFDMA waveform at 80 MHz with four 242-tone resource units, one user per RU. Use 4 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding, and MCS 5 for all users. Set APEPLength to 2000 bytes for each user. Generate the waveform with random payload bits and display the RU allocation. Print the PSDU lengths for all users.

assertions.description: Uses allocation index [192 192 192 192] for four 242-tone RUs at 80 MHz
Does not pass RU size 242 as allocation index
Generated code runs without error
Uses getPSDULength for HE-MU (not .PSDULength or psduLength)

evals.name: EHT-MU with MRU
evals.description: Tests EHT allocation indices, MRU construction, continuation indices, psduLength method, and per-user APEPLength sizing
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11be EHT-MU waveform at 80 MHz using a 484+242 Multi-Resource Unit (MRU) on subchannels 1, 2, and 4, with subchannel 3 as the gap. On subchannel 3 use a 106+26+106 layout. Use 2 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding. Set appropriate MCS and APEPLength values for each user considering the RU sizes (smaller RUs need smaller payloads). Generate with random bits and show the allocation.

assertions.description: Uses correct base index 112 for 484+242 MRU with SC3 independent
Uses continuation index 29 for 484-tone part
Uses continuation index 28 for 242-tone part
Generated code runs without error (especially no PPDU duration exceeded)
Uses psduLength() for EHT (not getPSDULength)

evals.name: Non-HT with MAC Frame
evals.description: Tests MAC frame construction: correct argument order, hex payload, frameLength from wlanMACFrame to set PSDULength, and multi-packet generation
evals.prompt: Generate a Non-HT (802.11a) waveform at 20 MHz, MCS 6, containing two packets separated by 20 microseconds of idle time. The first packet should contain a QoS Data MAC frame with hex payload 'A5B6C7D8E9F0A1B2C3D4'. The second packet should contain a Block Ack MAC frame. Use the frame length returned by wlanMACFrame to set the PSDULength of the PHY configuration. Print the PSDU length, MAC frame lengths, and total waveform duration.

assertions.description: Payload is the first argument to wlanMACFrame (not config first)
Sets PSDULength on the PHY config using the frame length from wlanMACFrame
Generated code runs without error
IdleTime specified in seconds (20e-6), not microseconds
Block Ack frame created without payload argument
Single-user payload is a plain vector, not wrapped in a cell array

evals.name: HE-MU OFDMA + MU-MIMO per Content Channel
evals.description: Tests per-content-channel MU-MIMO signaling on multi-subchannel RUs, mixed OFDMA layout, and spatial mapping
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11ax HE-MU waveform at 80 MHz with three RU groups: a 484-tone RU with 4 MU-MIMO users on subchannels 1-2, a 242-tone RU with 1 user on subchannel 3, and two 106-tone RUs with 1 user each on subchannel 4. Use 4 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding, MCS 7 for the 484-tone users, MCS 5 for the 242-tone user, and MCS 4 for the 106-tone users. Set APEPLength to 2000 for all users. Generate the waveform and show the allocation. Print the number of users, number of RUs, RU sizes, and all PSDU lengths.

assertions.description: Uses per-content-channel HE 484-tone indices (200-207 range) that sum to 4 MU-MIMO users across both subchannels
assertions.expected: Allocation indices for the 484-tone subchannels are in the 200-207 or 114 range and the total users on that RU is 4

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error — wrong per-CC indices would produce wrong user count or error
Uses getPSDULength for HE-MU
Sets SpatialMapping to a non-Direct mapping (Fourier, Hadamard, or Custom) for RUs where STS < NumTxAntennas
Total of 7 users (4 on 484 + 1 on 242 + 2 on 106+106)
Calls ruInfo(cfg) to verify the RU allocation and user counts after construction
Output shows correct RU sizes: one 484-tone, one 242-tone, and two 106-tone RUs
assertions.expected: RU sizes are 484, 242, 106, 106 (in any order) — 4 RUs total from ruInfo

evals.name: HE-MU Preamble Puncturing
evals.description: Tests that the agent uses allocation index 113 for preamble puncturing, not STAID=2046
evals.prompt: Generate a 160 MHz 802.11ax HE-MU waveform with 242-tone RUs on all subchannels except subchannel 2, which should be fully punctured (no preamble or data). Use 2 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding, MCS 5 for all active users, and APEPLength 2000. Generate the waveform and print how many active users there are.

assertions.description: Uses allocation index 113 for the punctured subchannel
Does not use STAID=2046 (which only disables user data, not the preamble)
Generated code runs without error
Uses getPSDULength for HE-MU
7 active users (8 subchannels minus 1 punctured)

evals.name: EHT TB Uplink with MRU
evals.description: Tests trigger-based config usage: wlanEHTTBConfig, RUSize/RUIndex properties, psduLength method, and OversamplingFactor
evals.prompt: Generate an EHT Trigger-Based uplink waveform at 80 MHz for a single user on a 242+484 MRU (RUSize [242 484], RUIndex [1 2]). Use MCS 7, 1 spatial stream, LDPC coding. Oversample by 1.5x. Print the PSDU length, sample rate, and waveform duration.

assertions.description: Uses wlanEHTTBConfig (not wlanEHTMUConfig)
Sets RUSize as a vector for MRU
Generated code runs without error
Uses psduLength() for EHT TB (not getPSDULength)
Passes OversamplingFactor to wlanWaveformGenerator
Single-user payload is a plain vector, not wrapped in a cell array

evals.name: EHT-MU 160 MHz with 996+484 MRU
evals.description: Tests 160 MHz EHT allocation indices, 996+484 MRU construction, continuation index layout, and puncturing index 26
evals.prompt: Generate a 160 MHz 802.11be EHT-MU waveform with a 996+484-tone MRU spanning subchannels 1-4 (996-tone) and 7-8 (484-tone), with 8 MU-MIMO users on the MRU. Place independent 242-tone RUs on subchannels 5 and 6 with 1 user each. Use 8 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding. Set MCS 9 and APEPLength 4000 for MRU users, MCS 5 and APEPLength 2000 for 242-tone users. Generate the waveform and show the allocation.

assertions.description: Uses base index 151 for 996+484 MRU with 8 users
Uses continuation index 30 for 996-tone subchannels
Uses continuation index 29 for 484-tone subchannels
Generated code runs without error
Uses psduLength() for EHT (not getPSDULength)
Calls ruInfo(cfg) to verify the MRU allocation and user counts after construction

evals.name: DSSS 802.11b Waveform
evals.description: Tests DSSS config: Modulation='DSSS', DataRate property, no OFDM properties, wlanSampleRate usage
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11b DSSS waveform at 11 Mbps with a 500-byte payload. Plot the time-domain waveform with a proper time axis. Print the sample rate, number of samples, and waveform duration.

assertions.description: Sets Modulation to DSSS
Uses DataRate property (not MCS)
Does not set ChannelBandwidth (DSSS does not use it)
Generated code runs without error (no invalid property errors)
Uses wlanSampleRate(cfg) for sample rate, not hardcoded 11e6
Single-user payload is a plain vector, not wrapped in a cell array

evals.name: EHT-MU Non-OFDMA MU-MIMO
evals.description: Tests non-OFDMA EHT MU-MIMO: string constructor with NumUsers, no allocation indices, psduLength
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11be EHT MU waveform with 2 users in a MU-MIMO configuration. Non-OFDMA. Channel bandwidth 80 MHz. Use 4 transmit antennas, 2 spatial streams per user, LDPC coding, MCS 9 for user 1 and MCS 7 for user 2. APEPLength 4000 for both users. Generate the waveform with random bits and print the waveform size, sample rate, and PSDU lengths.

assertions.description: Uses string constructor wlanEHTMUConfig with bandwidth string (not allocation indices)
Specifies NumUsers=2
Generated code runs without error
Uses psduLength() for EHT (not getPSDULength)

evals.name: HE-MU Non-OFDMA MU-MIMO
evals.description: Tests non-OFDMA HE MU-MIMO: full-band allocation indices with zero-user indices, getPSDULength, correct user count
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11ax HE-MU waveform with 3 MU-MIMO users sharing a single full-bandwidth 996-tone RU at 80 MHz. This should be non-OFDMA (all users on the same RU, no separate resource units). Use 4 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding. MCS 7 for user 1, MCS 5 for user 2, MCS 3 for user 3. APEPLength 3000 for all users. Generate the waveform with random bits and print the number of users and PSDU lengths.

assertions.description: Uses allocation index 210 (208 + 3 - 1 = 210 for 3 users on 996-tone)
Uses zero-user index 115 for remaining subchannels
Generated code runs without error
Uses getPSDULength for HE-MU (not psduLength)
Sets SpatialMapping to a non-Direct mapping (Fourier, Hadamard, or Custom) since total STS (3) < NumTxAntennas (4)
Calls ruInfo(cfg) to verify the 996-tone RU has exactly 3 users

evals.name: UHR MU OFDMA 80 MHz
evals.description: Tests UHR (802.11bn) OFDMA waveform: setupExample helper, uhrMUConfig with allocation indices, uhrWaveformGenerator, psduLength
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11bn (UHR / Wi-Fi 8) MU OFDMA waveform at 80 MHz with four 242-tone resource units, one user per RU. Use 4 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding. Use MCS 7, 8, 9, 10 for users 1 through 4. Set APEPLength to 4000 bytes for each user. Generate the waveform with random payload bits. Print the waveform size, transmit time, and PSDU lengths.

context.setup: Requires WLAN Toolbox with UHR example helpers

assertions.description: Loads UHR helper files via setupExample
Uses uhrMUConfig (not wlanEHTMUConfig or wlanHEMUConfig)
Uses uhrWaveformGenerator (not wlanWaveformGenerator)
Uses allocation index 64 for 242-tone RU (EHT scheme, not HE index 192)
Generated code runs without error

evals.name: UHR Non-OFDMA MU-MIMO
evals.description: Tests UHR non-OFDMA MU-MIMO: string constructor with NumUsers, psduLength
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11bn (UHR / Wi-Fi 8) non-OFDMA MU-MIMO waveform at 80 MHz with 2 users sharing a single full-bandwidth RU. Use 4 transmit antennas, 2 spatial streams per user (total 4 STS), LDPC coding. MCS 9 for user 1, MCS 7 for user 2. APEPLength 4000 for both. Generate with random bits and print the RU size, total STS, and waveform size.

context.setup: Requires WLAN Toolbox with UHR example helpers

assertions.description: Uses string constructor with bandwidth (not allocation indices)
Specifies NumUsers=2 in the uhrMUConfig constructor
Uses psduLength() for UHR (same as EHT)
Generated code runs without error

evals.name: UHR UEQM Per-Stream MCS
evals.description: Tests UHR Unequal Modulation (UEQM): MCS as a vector, decreasing order, same coding rate per stream, LDPC required
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11bn (UHR / Wi-Fi 8) OFDMA waveform at 80 MHz demonstrating UEQM (Unequal Modulation). Use four 242-tone RUs with 4 users. User 1: 2 spatial streams with different modulation per stream (1024QAM on stream 1, 256QAM on stream 2, both 5/6 coding rate). User 2: 3 spatial streams with decreasing modulation (256QAM, 64QAM, 16QAM, all 3/4 rate). User 3: 1 spatial stream, regular MCS 10. User 4: 4 spatial streams (1024QAM on streams 1-3, 256QAM on stream 4, all 5/6 rate). All users LDPC coding, APEPLength 4000. Use 4 transmit antennas with Fourier spatial mapping. After configuring each user, display the readonly UEQM, Modulation, and CodingRate properties to verify the per-stream configuration is correct. Generate the waveform.

context.setup: Requires WLAN Toolbox with UHR example helpers

assertions.description: Sets MCS as a vector for UEQM users (not scalar)
Uses LDPC coding (required for UEQM)
Uses uhrMUConfig for UHR UEQM
Uses uhrWaveformGenerator
Generated code runs without error (valid UEQM patterns)
Displays the readonly UEQM property to confirm UEQM is active for multi-stream users
Displays the readonly Modulation property showing per-stream modulation (e.g., 1024QAM, 256QAM)
Displays the readonly CodingRate property confirming same coding rate across streams

evals.name: UHR TB Uplink with DRU
evals.description: Tests UHR trigger-based uplink with Distributed RU: uhrTBConfig, DRU property, DistributionBandwidth, NumUHRLTFSymbols
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11bn (UHR / Wi-Fi 8) trigger-based uplink waveform at 80 MHz. Configure a single user on a 106-tone RU with Distributed RU (DRU) enabled using 20 MHz distribution bandwidth. Use 2 transmit antennas, 2 spatial streams, MCS 7, LDPC coding. Generate with random bits and print the channel bandwidth, RU size, DRU status, distribution bandwidth, and waveform size.

context.setup: Requires WLAN Toolbox with UHR example helpers

assertions.description: Uses uhrTBConfig (not wlanEHTTBConfig)
Uses DRU property (not DistributedRU)
Sets DistributionBandwidth to DBW20 for 20 MHz distribution
Sets NumUHRLTFSymbols to match spatial stream requirements
Generated code runs without error
Single-user payload is a plain vector, not wrapped in a cell array

evals.name: UHR LDPC2x and High MCS
evals.description: Tests UHR new channel coding (LDPC2x) and new MCS values (23 with 2/3 rate)
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11bn (UHR / Wi-Fi 8) waveform demonstrating new UHR features: an 80 MHz OFDMA waveform with four 242-tone RUs where users 1-3 use the new LDPC2x channel coding (MCS 11, 13, 23 respectively) and user 4 uses standard LDPC (MCS 9). 2 transmit antennas, 1 STS per user, APEPLength 3000. Print the waveform size and transmit time.

context.setup: Requires WLAN Toolbox with UHR example helpers

assertions.description: Uses LDPC2x channel coding
Uses uhrMUConfig for the waveform
Generated code runs without error

evals.name: EHT Non-OFDMA MU-MIMO with Puncturing
evals.description: Tests EHT non-OFDMA puncturing: PuncturedChannelFieldValue at construction, psduLength, multi-user cell array payload
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11be EHT MU-MIMO waveform at 80 MHz with 2 users sharing a single RU, but with one 20 MHz subchannel punctured (subchannel 2). Use 4 transmit antennas, 2 spatial streams per user, LDPC coding. MCS 9 for user 1, MCS 7 for user 2. APEPLength 4000 for both. Generate with random bits and print the channel bandwidth, puncturing pattern, PSDU lengths, waveform size, and transmit time.

assertions.description: Sets PuncturedChannelFieldValue at construction (read-only after)
Uses wlanEHTMUConfig with bandwidth string for non-OFDMA
Uses psduLength() for EHT (not getPSDULength)
Specifies NumUsers at construction for non-OFDMA MU-MIMO
Generated code runs without error
Puncturing pattern output shows subchannel 2 is punctured: [0 1 0 0] or [false true false false]
assertions.expected: PuncturingPattern is [0 1 0 0] or [false true false false] — only subchannel 2 is marked as punctured

evals.name: EHT DUP Mode
evals.description: Tests EHT DUP mode: EHTDUPMode set at construction, MCS 14 constraint, single user, psduLength, wlanSampleRate
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11be EHT DUP (duplicate) mode waveform at 80 MHz. EHT DUP duplicates the signal across subchannels for robust wide-bandwidth coverage. Use 1 transmit antenna, LDPC coding, and an APEP length of 300 bytes. Generate the waveform with random bits and print the channel bandwidth, EHT DUP mode status, PSDU length, sample rate, waveform size, and transmit time.

assertions.description: Enables EHTDUPMode at construction (not post-construction assignment)
Uses MCS 14 (the only valid MCS for EHT DUP mode)
Uses wlanEHTMUConfig with bandwidth string constructor (not allocation indices)
Uses psduLength() for EHT (not getPSDULength)
Generated code runs without error

evals.name: HE-MU 484-tone Per-Content-Channel User Split
evals.description: Tests that the agent correctly uses per-content-channel allocation indices for HE 484-tone RUs and verifies the configuration with ruInfo
evals.prompt: Generate an 802.11ax HE-MU waveform at 40 MHz with a single 484-tone RU carrying exactly 3 MU-MIMO users. The 484-tone RU spans 2 subchannels, and users are split across content channels. Use 4 transmit antennas, 1 spatial stream per user, LDPC coding, MCS 7 for all users, APEPLength 3000. After creating the config, verify the allocation with ruInfo — print the RU sizes, number of users per RU, and total user count to confirm exactly 3 users. Then generate the waveform and print the PSDU lengths.

assertions.expected: Allocation indices produce exactly 3 users on the 484-tone RU via content channel split

assertions.description: Calls ruInfo to verify the configuration
Output confirms exactly 3 users
Generated code runs without error — wrong indices would produce wrong user count
Uses getPSDULength for HE-MU (not psduLength)
Does not use the same index on both subchannels for 3 users (e.g., [201 201] gives 4 not 3)
